content.claim: Hungary operates one of the most selective education systems in Europe, with between-school variation in learning outcomes indicating social selection stronger than in most comparable European countries with early tracking systems.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Based on 2015 data, now a decade old
Measures between-school variation but does not isolate the 6/8-year track as the sole causal mechanism
Comparable data from other early-selection systems (Austria, Germany, Netherlands) also show high selection, suggesting systemic rather than track-specific drivers
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
